formulas.formula:
```json
[
  {
    "name": "Law of Independent Assortment",
    "formula": "Genes for different traits are sorted independently of each other",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describes the inheritance of genes independently of each other"
  },
  {
    "name": "Law of Segregation",
    "formula": "Each pair of alleles separates during gamete formation",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describes the inheritance of a single gene with two alleles"
  },
  {
    "name": "Law of Dominance",
    "formula": "One allele (dominant) will be expressed over the other allele (recessive)",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describes the relationship between a dominant and recessive allele"
  },
  {
    "name": "Law of Multiple Alleles",
    "formula": "More than two alleles can exist for a single gene",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describes the existence of multiple alleles for a single gene"
  },
  {
    "name": "Law of Incomplete Dominance",
    "formula": "Neither allele is completely dominant over the other",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describes the relationship between two alleles that do not show complete dominance"
  },
  {
    "name": "Law of Pleiotropy",
    "formula": "A single gene can affect multiple traits",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describes the effect of a single gene on multiple traits"
  },
  {
    "name": "Law of Polygenic Inheritance",
    "formula": "Multiple genes influence a single trait",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describes the influence of multiple genes on a single trait"
  },
  {
    "name": "Mitotic Equivalence",
    "formula": "All daughter cells are genetically identical to the parent cell",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describes the genetic similarity between daughter cells and the parent cell in mitosis"
  },
  {
    "name": "Somatic Cell Hybridization",
    "formula": "Hybridization of somatic cells from different species",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describes the technique of combining somatic cells from different species"
  },
  {
    "name": "Cytokinesis",
    "formula": "The process of dividing the cytoplasm during cell division",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Describes the process of dividing the cytoplasm during cell division"
  }
]
```

Note: This list includes key statements/laws related to genetics and cell biology. Since the chapter "Cell: The Unit of Life" does not involve complex mathematical formulas, the provided formulas/statements are based on key concepts and laws in genetics and cell biology.
